description: Medication formulary for Resident Life simulator - emergency medications with dosages, indications, and precautions

cardiac.name: Aspirin (ASA)
indications: Acute ischemic stroke (after 24h if tPA given)
cardiac.pearls: Chewable preferred for rapid absorption. Reduces mortality by 23% in STEMI (ISIS-2).

cardiac.name: Nitroglycerin
cardiac.pearls: Must wait 24h after sildenafil, 48h after tadalafil (profound hypotension risk).

cardiac.name: Unfractionated Heparin
cardiac.pearls: Monitor PTT Q6h. Target 1.5-2.5× normal (60-90 sec). Risk of HIT: 1-3%.

antibiotics.name: Ceftriaxone
antibiotics.pearls: Excellent CNS penetration (use 2g dose for meningitis). Covers S. pneumo, H. flu, E. coli, Klebsiella. Does NOT cover Pseudomonas, Enterococcus, anaerobes.

antibiotics.name: Piperacillin-Tazobactam
antibiotics.pearls: Covers Pseudomonas, anaerobes, Enterococcus (pip) + ESBL gram-negatives (tazo). Workhorse for sepsis. Does NOT cover MRSA or VRE.

antibiotics.name: Vancomycin
antibiotics.onset: 60 min (infuse over 60-90 min to avoid Red Man Syndrome)
antibiotics.pearls: Target trough 15-20 for severe infections. Red Man Syndrome from rapid infusion (histamine release). NOT absorbed PO (use PO only for C. diff).

antibiotics.name: Azithromycin
antibiotics.pearls: Added to beta-lactam for severe CAP. Also anti-inflammatory properties (reduced COPD exacerbations).

neuro.name: Alteplase (tPA)
indications: STEMI if PCI unavailable within 120 min
neuro.pearls: sICH risk: 6.4% (NINDS). 30% more likely to have minimal/no disability at 3 months. Strict BP control <180/105 for 24h post-tPA.

neuro.name: Lorazepam (Ativan)
neuro.dose: 0.1mg/kg IV (max 4mg/dose), repeat ×1 if ongoing at 5 min
neuro.pearls: Preferred benzo for status epilepticus. Longer duration than midazolam (6-8h vs 1-2h). Less respiratory depression than diazepam.

neuro.name: Nimodipine
neuro.pearls: ONLY intervention proven to improve outcomes in SAH (Class IA evidence). Not for hypertension. Mechanism: neuroprotection via vasodilation of cerebral vessels.

vasopressors.name: Norepinephrine (Levophed)
contraindications: Peripheral administration (can cause tissue necrosis if extravasation)
vasopressors.pearls: First-line vasopressor for septic shock (Surviving Sepsis Campaign). Target MAP ≥65. Add vasopressin if dose >0.3 mcg/kg/min.

vasopressors.name: Epinephrine (Adrenaline)
vasopressors.dose: Push: 1mg IV Q3-5min (cardiac arrest). Infusion: 2-10 mcg/min
contraindications: Caution in coronary artery disease (tachycardia, myocardial O2 demand)
vasopressors.pearls: In cardiac arrest: 1mg IV Q3-5min. In anaphylaxis: 0.3-0.5mg IM (anterolateral thigh), repeat Q5-15min. In shock: increases lactate (beta-2 stimulation of glycolysis).

resuscitation.name: Crystalloid (Lactated Ringer's / Normal Saline)
resuscitation.dose: 30mL/kg bolus for sepsis, 250-500mL boluses for hypovolemia
contraindications: Anuric renal failure with fluid overload
resuscitation.pearls: LR preferred over NS in large volumes (avoid hyperchloremic metabolic acidosis). 2L LR = same cost as 2L NS.

timingPenalties.tpaDelayed: Every 15 min delay = -5 reputation (time is brain)
timingPenalties.antibioticsDelayed: Every hour delay in sepsis = -10 reputation + mortality increase 7%
